title: Cleaning and housekeeping managers and supervisors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations. Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization, such as records and information management, mail distribution, and other office support services. (Based on 23 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct or coordinate the supportive services department of a business, agency, or organization, including administrative and operational services.
Develop operational standards and procedures for the work unit or department, including staffing, supply management, and service delivery.
Interview and hire new employees, and provide training and orientation to new staff.
Schedule work hours for employees and maintain records, including payroll and attendance.
Monitor quality of work and compliance with standards and regulations, conducting evaluations and taking corrective actions as needed.
Establish and implement marketing strategies and goals to promote services and manage customer relationships, including complaint resolution.
Identify skill development needs for staff and conduct training classes to ensure understanding of policies, procedures, and equipment usage.
Acquire, distribute, and store supplies, ensuring stock is maintained and vendors meet company requirements.
Analyze and evaluate operations to identify opportunities for improvement, cost reduction, and efficiency.
Comply with health and safety regulations and respond to emergencies to ensure the safety and well-being of staff and clients.
Communicate effectively with external vendors, service providers, and other departments to meet organizational needs and ensure service delivery.
Prepare and review operational reports and schedules to ensure efficiency and compliance with company policies.
Conduct market research and analyze trends to inform purchasing and operational decisions.
Manage budgeting processes for contracts, supplies, and staffing to optimize financial performance and resource allocation.
Inspect facilities and operational processes to ensure compliance with standards and timelines for maintenance and performance improvement.
Promote a positive organizational culture and environment, addressing performance issues and providing support to staff.
Coordinate with departmental leaders to align goals and strategies across the organization.
Develop and manage relationships with clients, addressing their needs and preferences while delivering services.
Conduct financial tasks such as preparing budgets and estimating costs related to operations and supplies.
Define roles and responsibilities for staff to facilitate effective service delivery according to operational needs.
Perform or assist with cleaning and maintenance duties as necessary to ensure operational efficiency and cleanliness.
Assign, supervise, and review the activities of fundraising and public relations staff, including managing fundraising budgets and creating effective communication plans.

Write interesting and effective press releases, prepare information for media kits, and develop and maintain company internet or intranet web pages. Additionally, produce films and other video products, regulate their distribution, and operate film library.
Plan and direct special events for fundraising to gain public attention through the media without advertising directly, such as silent auctions, dances, and community events.

Evaluate advertising and promotion programs for compatibility with public relations and fundraising efforts, and establish goals for soliciting funds. Additionally, develop strategies to encourage new or increased contributions.

Conduct research to identify the goals and charitable donation history of potential donors or investors, and establish effective working relationships with clients, government officials, and media representatives to develop new fundraising opportunities.
Draft speeches for company executives and arrange interviews or other forms of contact for them, while formulating policies and procedures related to fundraising and public information programs.
Monitor regulatory affairs trends related to environmental issues, evaluate and ensure compliance with fundraising and public relations policies, and provide training on compliance-related topics.
Identify and apply for project funding, and maintain company archives along with documentation of compliance and fundraising activities.
Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events, and develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs to improve community health initiatives.
Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work and adherence to safety regulations or policies, and oversee the maintenance of fundraising or wellness event equipment.
Advise on the development of loss-investigation procedures, direct installation of surveillance equipment, and investigate or interview individuals suspected of theft.
Maintain databases related to security, monitor paperwork procedures to prevent shortages, and perform audits and deposit investigations for accurate cash accounting.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations, monitor emerging trends regarding regulatory compliance, and manage activities such as audits or inspections.
Develop and implement plans for wellness and fitness programs, train staff in these areas, and ensure compliance with health and hygiene standards.
Organize events such as health screenings or fitness classes and manage facilities to ensure safety and cleanliness.
Prepare and submit regulatory applications and review submission materials for compliance with agency requirements.
Develop and implement marketing strategies, including the sale of products or services, and maintain client databases.
Train staff in regulatory policies, assess performance, and communicate job responsibilities and client service standards.
Establish budgets for projects or departments and prepare operational plans to meet financial goals.
Recruit, interview, or hire employees and inventory products or supplies as necessary for operations.
